skill.name: extract-design-system
skill.description: Extract design primitives from a public website and generate starter token files for your project.
skill.summary: Turn a public website into starter design token artifacts for review and reuse.
skill.author: arvindrk

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected many command and weak-crypto patterns, but review found no prompt injection, malicious intent, network exfiltration, or real cryptographic code. The external-command findings are valid because the skill tells agents to run npx-based tools, so publication is acceptable only with a warning about user approval and package trust.

medium_findings.title: External package execution through npx commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run npx playwright install chromium and npx extract-design-system commands. This matches the stated workflow, but it executes external packages and should require user approval and package trust review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command text is directly present in the scanned files and would execute package-manager commands. The commands are consistent with the skill purpose and are not evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography static findings dismissed
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography matches appear to be false positives from markdown content and file references. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithms in the reviewed lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are short markdown instructions and output descriptions, not executable cryptographic code. Manual review did not find any cryptographic API usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx command execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses npx commands to install Chromium support and run the extractor. This is a legitimate implementation path but remains a dangerous pattern because package execution can run third-party code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact npx commands are visible in the workflow. Risk depends on package integrity and whether the user approves execution.

content.value_statement: Teams often need a fast starting point when translating an existing website into project design tokens. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through extraction, review, and starter token generation.
actual_capabilities: Confirms the target public website URL before extraction.
Runs the extractor workflow for public website design primitives.
Generates or reviews raw and normalized extraction artifacts.
Creates starter tokens in JSON and CSS formats.
Summarizes colors, fonts, spacing, radii, and shadows when detected.
Separates extraction-only work from starter file generation.
limitations: It does not create a complete component library.
It does not guarantee pixel-perfect reproduction of a website.
It depends on a public and reachable target URL.
It requires review before using extracted output as authoritative.

use_cases.title: Start a redesign audit
use_cases.description: Extract visible colors, typography, and spacing from a public site before planning a redesign.

use_cases.title: Bootstrap frontend tokens
use_cases.description: Generate starter CSS variables and token JSON for a new frontend project.

use_cases.title: Compare brand implementation
use_cases.description: Review extracted primitives against brand guidelines to find drift or missing token coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Extract basic tokens
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single public URL.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract design primitives from this public website: [URL]. Summarize the likely colors, fonts, spacing, radii, and shadows.

prompt_templates.title: Generate starter files
prompt_templates.scenario: Create reusable token artifacts after extraction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the extraction for [URL] and generate starter token files. Explain each generated artifact before I use it.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze without writing files
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze [URL] with extraction-only mode. Do not create starter token files. Report the design primitives you find.

prompt_templates.title: Regenerate from normalized output
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow using an existing normalized artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the existing .extract-design-system/normalized.json file to regenerate starter token files. Do not modify app code or styles.

output_examples.input: Extract design primitives from a public marketing site.
output_examples.output: A summary of likely primary, secondary, and accent colors, detected fonts, and available spacing, radius, and shadow scales.

output_examples.input: Generate starter token files from a reachable product page.
output: Starter CSS variables for frontend use.

output_examples.input: Regenerate token files from an existing normalized artifact.
output_examples.output: Updated starter token artifacts based on the current normalized extraction, with no app code changes unless explicitly approved.

best_practices: Use only public websites that you are allowed to inspect.
Review extracted tokens before importing them into an application.
Ask before modifying existing app code, styles, or configuration.
anti_patterns: Do not treat one page as a complete product design system.
Do not overwrite an existing design system without confirmation.
Do not trust third-party website content or extracted output without review.

faq.question: Does this skill build a full design system?
faq.answer: No. It extracts starter primitives and token files, not complete components or design governance.

faq.question: Can it inspect private websites?
faq.answer: The documented workflow expects a public, reachable URL. Private sites may need separate access setup.

faq.question: What files can it produce?
faq.answer: It can work with raw extraction output, normalized output, token JSON, and starter CSS variables.

faq.question: Does it modify application code?
faq.answer: It tells the agent to ask before changing existing app code, styles, or configuration files.

faq.question: Why does it use Playwright?
faq.answer: The workflow uses Chromium support so the extractor can inspect rendered website styles.

faq.question: Should extracted tokens be trusted immediately?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that extracted output is untrusted third-party input until reviewed.
